fix(player): 避免点歌时主线程卡死

将解码缓冲改为有界队列，并限制每个 tick 提交到 OpenAL 的 buffer 数量，避免点歌后一次性在主线程清空整首歌的缓冲队列。\n\n同时回收已处理的 OpenAL buffer，让播放改为稳定的流式推进。

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add URL-controlled translation presets to fna2faa

Support loading nucleotide input from the query string, selecting a specific one-frame translation or six-frame translation via the translate parameter, and toggling stop-at-first-stop from the URL. Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Package overlay icons and add card workflow metadata

Publish overlay SVG assets with a package manifest so UI can resolve metadata-defined icons without a sibling source checkout. Normalize asset icon paths to packaged overlay-icons locations and expose new package exports for the icon manifest and raw icon files.

Register AddCard as a real create-surface workflow launcher with generic asset-upload metadata and explicit create-items submission bindings for card face and back uploads. Add built-in selection overlay actions for common board and text operations, and document the packaged integration contract in the new UI agent handout.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Move built-in overlay icons next to their owning items

Localize built-in overlay icon assets so metadata no longer depends on a central core sprite for normal UI rendering.

This commit:
- adds item-local SVG icon assets for drawing, connector, shape, BPMN, frame, rich text, dice, screen, deck, and card overlays
- rewires built-in overlay metadata to reference those local assets directly
- preserves dynamic swatch/state hints for fill, stroke, text-size, and tool-color icons
- keeps symbol support in the overlay contract for extensibility, while making current built-ins asset-first
- adds a new UI integration handout that matches the metadata and icon structure now present in core
- marks older handouts as superseded and removes the outdated icon-only handout
- cleans up rebase fallout in the shape and rich-text overlay files

The result is a cleaner plugin boundary: icons now live with the item families that own them, and the UI can render built-in overlays directly from core-provided assets and metadata.

Build verification: bun run build

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

[raster] Clamp reflected gradient LUT coordinates

Malformed reflected gradients can produce very large finite
gradient parameters. The raster backend normalized reflect
mode by casting floorf(t) to int, which can overflow and
drive LUT lookups past the fixed-size stack buffer.

Fold reflected coordinates with fmodf(), clamp LUT inputs
defensively, and add the minimized oss-fuzz raster
testcase to the corpus.

Fixes: https://oss-fuzz.com/testcase-detail/6597066439852032
Tested: meson test -C build
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Extend overlay metadata for universal create/edit surfaces

Expand the core overlay contract so the UI can build creation and editing surfaces from library-provided metadata instead of relying on hardcoded item knowledge.

This commit:
- adds create-surface grouping and launch metadata for tool families
- adds lightweight applicability conditions and logical action sections
- adds presentation hints for picker variants and quick-option subsets
- adds a generic asset-upload editor for image/media/card-style workflows
- adds create-surface and condition helpers to the overlay registry
- wires built-in tools and item overlays into the richer contract
- introduces a core-owned overlay icon pack and sprite asset
- rewires built-in overlay icons to point at core-provided symbols
- adds a new universal UI handout and updates icon documentation

The result is a stronger plugin-ready boundary where this repo owns item-specific behavior descriptions and the icon assets that go with them, while the UI stays generic and focuses on rendering.

Build verification: bun run build

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(deps): consolidate Dependabot Go module updates (#1098)

### Motivation
- Reduce Dependabot noise by rolling up multiple open, passing
Dependabot Go module updates into a single, non-breaking dependency bump
PR.
- Keep the module graph consistent and resolve transitive changes so
local development and CI see the same versions.
- Validate changes using the repository Makefile-native workflow and
sign off with the project DCO policy.

### Description
- Applied the Dependabot-targeted minor/patch bumps and compatible
transitive updates in `go.mod`/`go.sum`, including (not exhaustive)
`github.com/cli/cli/v2` → `v2.89.0`, `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` →
`v0.47.0`, `github.com/yuin/goldmark` → `v1.8.2`, `golang.org/x/oauth2`
→ `v0.36.0`, `google.golang.org/api` → `v0.274.0`, `golang.org/x/net` →
`v0.52.0`, `github.com/fatih/color` → `v1.19.0`,
`github.com/go-playground/validator/v10` → `v10.30.2`, and
`go.opentelemetry.io/contrib/instrumentation/net/http/otelhttp` →
`v0.67.0`.
- Updated `go.sum` via dependency resolution and ran `go mod tidy` to
clean the module graph and checksum file.
- Performed dependency upgrades with `go get -t -u ./...` and recorded
the resulting module set changes in `go.mod`/`go.sum`.
- Changes were committed with DCO signoff and are available in the
working branch at commit `ae9bcfefedf486335008047cf8ea787beaea8f1b`.

### Testing
- Ran `go get -t -u ./...` which completed and upgraded direct and
transitive modules successfully.
- Ran `go mod tidy` which completed and normalized `go.mod`/`go.sum`
successfully.
- Ran `make install/dev` which completed (tool install steps ran, though
the environment lacks a git tag description and some tool binaries may
be placed under the container GOPATH).
- Ran `make lint` which failed due to a missing vet tool binary: `go vet
-vettool=/root/go/bin/checklocks ...` could not find
`/root/go/bin/checklocks` in this environment.
- Ran `make test` which failed in this container due to
environment/tooling/runtime expectations (examples include missing
`deno`, interpreters/tooling for some file-command tests producing empty
output, and tests asserting specific version/frontmatter metadata that
differ in this ephemeral environment).

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69d3fbb6c0dc8324a9114e8e04cb0eab)

Signed-off-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Signed-off-by: Sebastian (Tiedtke) Huckleberry <sebastiantiedtke@gmail.com>

commit.message:
fix(import): handle phone uniqueness conflicts + speed up preview progress (#1093)

* fix(import): handle phone uniqueness conflicts during order commit + speed up preview progress

Two fixes for the import job pipeline:

1. Customer phone dedup (resolve-import-customer.ts): Fix customers_merchant_phone_unique
   constraint violation during order commit. Phone-only orders now link to existing phone
   holders with email/user_id. Email-identified orders INSERT with phone=null when phone
   is already taken. Safety net retries INSERT without phone on concurrent 23505 errors.

2. Preview progress cold-start (import-job-service.ts): Report totalRows to the UI as
   soon as CSV is parsed, before existing orders/products DB queries finish. This closes
   the gap where the UI shows 0/0 while waiting for unrelated DB loads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(import): improve retry error message and fix phone cache corruption

- Include original insertError.message as fallback when retryError is null
- Only update customersByPhone cache using the actual inserted phone value,
  not the original order phone (which may have been nulled out to avoid
  the uniqueness constraint)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(import): tighten phone match guard and handle phone-only concurrent collisions

- Change allPhoneMatches guard from length > 0 to === 1 to avoid ambiguous merges
- For phone-only orders hitting 23505, look up existing phone holder instead of
  inserting a null-phone row that would lose the phone identifier
- Email-identified orders still retry INSERT with phone=null as before

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(docs): align publishing flow with DocsQuartz deployments (#2)

Document the two-repository publishing pipeline and add the source-repo
trigger that tells DocsQuartz to rebuild from docs/main. This keeps docs
as the source of truth while making the operational setup explicit for
GitHub Pages, secrets, and custom-domain wiring.

Constraint: docs must remain the source of truth with no mirrored content commits
Constraint: production deploys must trigger only from docs/main pushes
Rejected: Commit synchronized docs content into DocsQuartz | would create drift and duplicate source ownership
Rejected: Add PR preview deployment now | explicitly out of scope for this change
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: If DocsQuartz default branch changes, keep the dispatch target workflow on that default branch or repository_dispatch will stop firing
Tested: YAML parse for docs workflow; Prettier check for new docs files; end-to-end Quartz temp build using docs content
Not-tested: Live GitHub Actions dispatch against production secrets; DNS/custom-domain propagation on docs.smart-class.org

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(push): explicit params to fix stale closure + remove Device.isDevice guard (#1095)

* fix(push): pass explicit userId/merchantId to avoid stale closures

React Compiler memoization causes registerPush() closures to capture
stale user/merchant values. Pass them explicitly from the layout effects
where the values are known to be current.

Also removes Device.isDevice guard that falsely blocks real TestFlight
devices when expo-device is dynamically imported.

commit.message:
Work (#9)

* Add contacts import (Google Sheets/YClients), DB migrations, and clients/staff/settings UI/hooks updates

### Motivation
- Provide a robust path to import contacts from either YClients or Google Sheets and persist import metadata in org settings.
- Make clients UI more feature-rich (age/discount filters, wider table with left-accent scrollbar) and keep staff UI in sync with real masters data from the DB.
- Ensure database schema supports YClients-compatible client fields and automatic linking between user records and clients when YClients IDs appear.

### Description
- Added an API endpoint `app/api/clients/import/route.ts` that normalizes rows from `yclients` or `google_sheets`, upserts/inserts into `clients`, and writes import metadata into `org_settings`.
- Implemented `lib/imports/normalizeGoogleSheetsClient.ts` to adapt Google Sheets rows into the clients schema and added `normalizeYclientsRow` logic in the import route.
- Introduced DB migrations and helper SQL: `add_contacts_import_source.sql`, `add_yclients_clients_columns.sql`, `link_users_to_clients_on_yc_id.sql`, seed and reset updates and a seeds file `seed_test_masters.sql` to populate masters for staff cards.
- Updated frontend: extended clients filters and table (`app/clients/page.tsx`) with age/discount filters, redesigned table layout and local left-accent scrollbar (`app/globals.css`), added settings UI to pick and save import source (`app/settings/page.tsx`), and disabled/marked in-development auto-systems in `app/system/page.tsx`.
- Refactored data hooks: `lib/hooks/useClients.ts` now reads from `clients` and normalizes new fields (discount, channels, birthday, etc.), and added a new `lib/hooks/useYclientsMasters.ts` hook to fetch/subscribe to `masters` for the staff UI; `app/staff/page.tsx` updated to consume masters and render realistic KPIs and cards.

### Testing
- Ran TypeScript/Next.js build (`next build`) and static type-checking locally and the build completed without type errors.
- Ran linter/format checks (ESLint) and no lint errors were reported for modified files.
- Performed basic automated smoke checks by running the dev server and loading `Clients`, `Staff`, and `Settings` pages to ensure hooks and new components render without runtime errors (no automated unit tests were changed or present for these new features).

* Store messenger user IDs on clients during yc_id auto-link

* Add reverse sync from clients to messenger user tables

---------

perf: checkpoint optimization experiments

Summary:
- checkpoint flow-core profiling, runtime scheduling, and pipeline line-spawn work
- capture flow-algorithms sort tuning, docs/plans, and benchmark reports
- ignore local .codex/ state and keep it out of version control

Rationale:
- preserve the current optimization worktree across pipeline, DAG, and sort experiments
- keep all-serial pipeline token ordering intact while retaining global line spawn for mixed pipelines

Validation:
- cargo test -p flow-core
- cargo test -p flow-algorithms
- full workspace validation not run

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: RustFlow
repository.description: A Rust version Taskflow

commit.message:
Phase 1: Implement comprehensive validators per final_plan.md sections 4.1-4.4

- validate_password: 4-50 chars, no spaces (final_plan.md 4.1.2)
- validate_date_plan: YYYY-MM-DD/YYYY.MM.DD/YYYY MM DD with no mixing, 0-padded, valid calendar dates (final_plan.md 4.2.1)
- validate_time_plan: 09:00 or 18:00 only, HH:MM or HHMM format (final_plan.md 4.2.2)
- validate_equipment_serial: [TYPE]-[NUMBER] pattern, types NB/PJ/WC/CB, numbers 001-003 (final_plan.md 4.3.2)
- validate_reason: 0-20 chars, no newlines, empty string allowed (final_plan.md 4.4)

Added 87 comprehensive test cases covering:
- All grammar rules and semantic validations per plan
- Edge cases: leap years, month-end dates, separator mixing, padding, boundary values
- All valid equipment types and serial numbers
- Error message validation for user feedback

All tests passing (87/87). No LSP diagnostics errors.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs: summarize optimization progress and next plan

Summary:\n- add a one-page overview section for current progress and next execution plan\n- document retained pipeline baseline vs rejected follow-up experiments\n- clarify priority order and execution gate criteria

Rationale:\n- make decision context and execution order immediately visible without scanning the whole plan\n- align follow-up work on P0/P2/P3 while keeping P1 evidence-driven

Validation:\n- documentation update only\n- no code/tests run in this commit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
